polls.text: North Carolina U.S. Senate likely voter ballot test, June 1-10 2026, Catawba College/YouGov. Candidate A is the Republican, Michael Whatley; candidate B is the Democrat, Roy Cooper.

Texas U.S. Senate topline ballot test, April 17-20 2026. Candidate A is the Republican, Ken Paxton; candidate B is the Democrat, James Talarico. Topline-only row because Party ID crosstabs were not available in the reviewed public source.

Texas governor topline ballot test, April 17-20 2026. Candidate A is the Republican, Greg Abbott; candidate B is the Democrat, Gina Hinojosa. Topline-only row because Party ID crosstabs were not available in the reviewed public source.

Ohio governor topline ballot test, April 3-9 2026, Echelon Insights for NetChoice. Candidate A is the Republican, Vivek Ramaswamy; candidate B is the Democrat, Amy Acton. Topline-only row because the public PDF did not publish Party ID crosstabs.

Ohio U.S. Senate topline ballot test, April 3-9 2026, Echelon Insights for NetChoice. Candidate A is the Republican, Jon Husted; candidate B is the Democrat, Sherrod Brown. Topline-only row because the public PDF did not publish Party ID crosstabs.

Texas governor likely voter ballot test, June 21 2026, SoCal Strategies for Red Eagle Politics. Candidate A is the Republican, Greg Abbott; candidate B is the Democrat, Gina Hinojosa. Party ID crosstabs use the linked public workbook's Q5/Q6 Governor table with undecided leaners allocated.

Texas U.S. Senate likely voter ballot test, June 21 2026, SoCal Strategies for Red Eagle Politics. Candidate A is the Republican, Ken Paxton; candidate B is the Democrat, James Talarico. Party ID crosstabs use the linked public workbook's Q7/Q8 Senate table with undecided leaners allocated.

Candidate A is the Republican, Susan Collins; candidate B is the Democrat, Graham Platner. Topline-only row from the RealClearPolling Collins-vs-Platner polling page and public report; clear party-ID crosstabs with subgroup sample sizes were not available in reviewed public sources.

Party-ID crosstabs are from the Fox public crosstab PDF; subgroup sample sizes are estimated from Fox's published party-ID subgroup MOEs because exact subgroup Ns were not printed.

Iowa governor registered-voter ballot test, June 23-27 2026, Fox News. Candidate A is the Republican, Zach Lahn; candidate B is the Democrat, Rob Sand. Party-ID crosstabs are from the Fox public crosstab PDF; subgroup sample sizes are estimated from Fox's published party-ID subgroup MOEs because exact subgroup Ns were not printed.

Party-ID crosstabs are from the Fox public crosstab PDF; subgroup sample sizes are estimated from Fox's published party-ID subgroup MOEs because exact subgroup Ns were not printed.

Georgia governor registered-voter ballot test, June 23-27 2026, Fox News. Candidate A is the Republican, Rick Jackson; candidate B is the Democrat, Keisha Lance Bottoms. Maine U.S. Senate registered-voter ballot test, June 23-27 2026, Fox News. Candidate A is the Republican, Susan Collins; candidate B is the Democrat, Graham Platner. RCP latest-polls row and Fox release show Collins 50, Platner 47. Topline-only row because clear party-ID subgroup sample sizes were not available in the reviewed public source.

Iowa U.S. Senate likely-voter ballot test, June 16-19 2026, Cygnal/Iowans for Tax Relief Foundation. Candidate A is the Republican, Ashley Hinson; candidate B is the Democrat, Josh Turek. Source PDF shows Hinson 46.0, Turek 44.2. Topline-only row because the reviewed topline PDF did not provide party-ID ballot crosstabs.

Pennsylvania governor likely-voter ballot test, June 18-25 2026, Morning Scrapple/PennLive/Bravo Group. Candidate A is the Republican, Stacy Garrity; candidate B is the Democrat, Josh Shapiro. RCP latest-polls row shows Shapiro 54, Garrity 29; Morning Scrapple source page gives the June field dates and n=644. Topline-only row because public party-ID crosstabs were not available in the reviewed source.

Ohio U.S. Senate likely-voter ballot test, June 14-16 2026, AARP/Fabrizio Ward/Impact Research. Candidate A is the Republican, Jon Husted; candidate B is the Democrat, Sherrod Brown. RCP latest-polls row shows Brown 48, Husted 45; AARP source page gives the June field dates and n=800. Topline-only row because public party-ID crosstabs were not available in the reviewed source.

Ohio governor likely-voter ballot test, June 14-16 2026, AARP/Fabrizio Ward/Impact Research. Candidate A is the Republican, Vivek Ramaswamy; candidate B is the Democrat, Amy Acton. RCP latest-polls row shows Acton 47, Ramaswamy 44; AARP source page gives the June field dates and n=800. Topline-only row because public party-ID crosstabs were not available in the reviewed source.

Iowa governor ballot test, June 15-27 2026, New York Times/Siena College. Candidate A is the Republican, Zach Lahn; candidate B is the Democrat, Rob Sand. RCP latest-polls row shows Sand 48, Lahn 47. Topline-only row; the sample size uses the already-ingested Iowa NYT/Siena party-ID crosstab total for the same state poll because a separate governor topline sample size was not visible in the static RCP extract.

Ohio governor ballot test, June 15-28 2026, New York Times/Siena College. Candidate A is the Republican, Vivek Ramaswamy; candidate B is the Democrat, Amy Acton. RCP latest-polls row shows Acton 47, Ramaswamy 47. Topline-only row; the sample size uses the already-ingested Ohio NYT/Siena party-ID crosstab total for the same state poll because a separate governor topline sample size was not visible in the static RCP extract.

Texas governor ballot test, June 19-27 2026, New York Times/Siena College. Candidate A is the Republican, Greg Abbott; candidate B is the Democrat, Gina Hinojosa. RCP latest-polls row shows Abbott 51, Hinojosa 44. Topline-only row; the sample size uses the already-ingested Texas NYT/Siena party-ID crosstab total for the same state poll because a separate governor topline sample size was not visible in the static RCP extract.
